description_long: This object is a large, prehistoric-looking bird with an elongated, pointed beak and a prominent, upward-curving crest on its head. Its wings are long, narrow, and angular, with a leathery texture and a gradient of brown and gray hues, suggesting adaptations for soaring flight. The body is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, which is short and appears wedge-shaped. The legs are thin and tucked close to the body, with a blue-gray coloration and small, clawed feet. The head and crest are marked with reddish-orange tones, contrasting with the more muted colors of the wings and body. The overall appearance is reminiscent of a pterosaur, specifically a Pteranodon, rather than a modern bird.
description: A large, prehistoric bird with a pointed beak, prominent crest, long angular wings, and a wedge-shaped tail. Its coloration features brown and gray wings with reddish-orange highlights on the head and crest.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated beak, prominent crest, and the full profile of the angular wings and streamlined body.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the wide wingspan, narrow body, and the symmetrical shape of the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the crest and beak visible, and the wing structure clearly outlined.
description_view_3: Rear view emphasizing the wedge-shaped tail, the tapering body, and the full extension of both wings.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Solid and gradient coloration with muted brown and gray on wings and body, reddish-orange crest and head, no distinct spots or stripes.
